arcs.title: Northeast by Nightfall
arcs.summary: Day 3: The Hempworth chart was fully unfolded Wednesday morning on the kitchen table. Christine traced the northeastern route to the edge. Both brass ducks are now facing northeast, having completed their turn unobserved between seven-nineteen and seven twenty-two. Steve has written three marginal notations; the third remains untranslated. The Jeep has shifted orientation. No departure date has been named, but the morning had the quality of one in which it could be. Day 4 should deliver the resolution: a departure date named, the route committed to, and the northeast destination visited — or the naming of the date itself as the arrival of a settled thing, with the journey implicit and certain.
